---
title: "Across Cell Variability"
author: "Atreya Choudhury"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output:
  bookdown::html_document2:
    code_folding: hide
    number_sections: no
    fig_crop: yes
---

## Strategy

The goal is to generate features with various heterogeneity levels for the purpose of across cell differentiation.
The goal here is to focus more on  the stochastic nature of methylation.

Our strategy here is to mix markov chains generated using a transition matrix of the form
$$
  \begin{bmatrix}
  \rho & 1 - \rho \\
  \rho & 1 - \rho
  \end{bmatrix}
$$
with different values of $\rho$ to create more or less heterogeneity across samples in a region.

We start by creating a template for a cell.
We have a fixed number of features, the length of each feature is taken to be the same and the number of simulated CpG sites is the same as well.
Every feature is given an **across cell Variability Index (acVI)** between 1 and 10 and stored in the `bc_vi` column.
We have a list of 10 equally spaced values of $\rho$ from $0.1$ to $0.9$.
Now, for a feature with given variability index, say $x$, we take the first $x$ elements from our list of $\rho$ values and sample from it to generate $\rho$ values for the samples belonging to that feature.
These sampled values of $\rho$ are then stored in the `rho` column.

With the template, we now generate samples as described above.
At every intersection of sample and feature, we have a value of $\rho$ which can be used to sample a markov chain.
We observe that features with a lower acVI see more homogenous samplings in `rho` which lead to lesser shannon entropy and vice-versa.
Also observe that chains generated with higher values of $\rho$ are also more methylated.
This leads to a relationship between average methylation and `bc_vi`, which is visible from scMET's performance.

```{r, setup}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggpubr)
  library(svglite)
})

source('rules/src/ggtheme.R')

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    echo = FALSE,
    message = FALSE,
    warning = FALSE,
    fig.path = "simulations/output/06_plots/",
    dev = c("png", "svg"),
    dpi = 500,
    fig.width  = ng_fig_size(1, 1)$w,
    fig.height = ng_fig_size(1, 1)$h
)

log_con <- file(snakemake@log[[1]], open = "wt")
sink(log_con)
sink(log_con, type = "message")

source("rules/src/mi_table.R")
source("rules/src/make_grid_plots.R")

data_dir <- dirname(snakemake@input[["disp_scmet"]])
S_GRID <- snakemake@params[["S_GRID"]]
N_GRID <- snakemake@params[["N_GRID"]]
DISP <- snakemake@params[["DISP"]]

## Across-cell colour gradient (purple)
ac_scale <- scale_color_gradient(low = "grey90", high = "#800080")
```

## Variation
For benchmarking against the number of samples, we used a grid with `r length(S_GRID$S)` sample sizes (S = `r paste(S_GRID$S, collapse = ", ")`), `r S_GRID$N` features where each feature has `r S_GRID$f` CpGs (all covered).
```{r}
s_plots <- make_grid_plots(S_GRID, "S", "bc_vi", data_dir)
```

For benchmarking against the number of features, we used a grid with `r N_GRID$S` samples, `r length(N_GRID$N)` feature sizes (N = `r paste(N_GRID$N, collapse = ", ")`) where each feature has `r N_GRID$f` CpGs (all covered).
```{r}
n_plots <- make_grid_plots(N_GRID, "N", "bc_vi", data_dir)
```

## One Example

We show one example with `r DISP$S` samples, `r DISP$N` features where each feature has `r DISP$f` CpGs (all covered).
```{r}
S <- DISP$S
N <- DISP$N
f <- DISP$f

intervals <- read.table(
  paste0(data_dir, paste("/intervals", S, N, f, "tsv", sep = ".")),
  header = T
)
intervals <- intervals["bc_vi"]
sampens <- read.table(
  paste0(data_dir, paste("/yamet", S, N, f, "det.out", sep = ".")),
  header = T
)
jnt <- cbind(sampens, intervals)
rownames(jnt) <- paste(jnt$start, jnt$end, sep = ":")
output_cols <- grep("\\.tsv$", colnames(jnt), value = TRUE)
jnt$sampen_avg <- rowMeans(jnt[, output_cols])
jnt.long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(output_cols, function(col) {
  data.frame(
    start = jnt$start,
    end = jnt$end,
    sample = col,
    sampen = jnt[[col]],
    shannon = jnt$shannon,
    avg_meth = jnt$avg_meth,
    bc_vi = jnt$bc_vi
  )
}))

meth <- read.table(
  paste0(data_dir, paste("/yamet", S, N, f, "meth.out", sep = ".")),
  header = TRUE
)
meth.long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(output_cols, function(col) {
  data.frame(
    meth = meth[[col]]
  )
}))

all.long <- cbind(jnt.long, meth.long)

samp_meth <- ggplot(all.long, aes(x = meth, y = sampen, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = "sample entropy", color = "acVI"
  )

samp_vi <- ggplot(all.long, aes(x = as.factor(bc_vi), y = sampen)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, position = position_jitter(width = 0.2)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.3, position = position_dodge(0.8)) +
  theme_ng() +
  labs(
    x = "acVI", y = "sample entropy"
  )

shannon_meth <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = avg_meth, y = shannon, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = "Shannon entropy", color = "acVI"
  )

shannon_vi <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = as.factor(bc_vi), y = shannon, color = avg_meth)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  labs(
    x = "acVI", y = "Shannon entropy", color = "avg. methylation"
  )

samp_shan <- ggplot(all.long, aes(x = shannon, y = sampen, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "Shannon entropy", y = "sample entropy", color = "acVI"
  )
```

```{r}
scmet_fit <- readRDS(paste0(data_dir, paste("/scmet", S, N, f, "rds", sep = ".")))
tmp <- colMeans(scmet_fit$posterior$mu)
jnt$scmet_mu <- tmp[match(rownames(jnt), names(tmp))]
tmp <- colMeans(scmet_fit$posterior$gamma)
jnt$scmet_gamma <- tmp[match(rownames(jnt), names(tmp))]
tmp <- colMeans(scmet_fit$posterior$epsilon)
jnt$scmet_epsilon <- tmp[match(rownames(jnt), names(tmp))]
```

```{r}
scmet_mu_vi <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = as.factor(bc_vi), y = scmet_mu, color = avg_meth)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  labs(
    x = "acVI", y = expression("scMET " ~ mu), color = "avg. methylation"
  )

scmet_mu_meth <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = avg_meth, y = scmet_mu, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = expression("scMET " ~ mu), color = "acVI"
  )

scmet_gamma_meth <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = avg_meth, y = scmet_gamma, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = expression("scMET " ~ gamma), color = "acVI"
  )

scmet_epsilon_meth <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = avg_meth, y = scmet_epsilon, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = expression("scMET " ~ epsilon), color = "acVI"
  )

sampen_avg_vi <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = as.factor(bc_vi), y = sampen_avg, color = avg_meth)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  labs(
    x = "acVI", y = "avg. sample entropy", color = "avg. methylation"
  )

sampen_avg_meth <- ggplot(jnt, aes(x = avg_meth, y = sampen_avg, color = bc_vi)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_ng() +
  ac_scale +
  labs(
    x = "avg. methylation", y = "avg. sample entropy", color = "acVI"
  )
```

```{r}
mi_table <- mi_table_gen(jnt, "bc_vi", table = T)
mi_fd <- mi_table_gen(jnt, "bc_vi", table = F)
saveRDS(mi_fd,     'simulations/rds/perf_across.rds')
saveRDS(all.long,  'simulations/rds/all.long_across.rds')
saveRDS(jnt,       'simulations/rds/jnt_across.rds')
saveRDS(s_plots,   'simulations/rds/s_plots_across.rds')
saveRDS(n_plots,   'simulations/rds/n_plots_across.rds')
```

```{r adj-across}
## ── Adjusted entropy (adjS = norm. sample entropy, adjH = norm. Shannon) ──────

## Load norm.det.out for the display example
norm_det <- read.table(
  paste0(data_dir, paste("/yamet", S, N, f, "norm.det.out", sep = ".")),
  header = T
)
rownames(norm_det) <- paste(norm_det$start, norm_det$end, sep = ":")
norm_output_cols <- grep("\\.tsv$", colnames(norm_det), value = TRUE)

## Per-cell per-feature long format with adj columns
jnt.adj.long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(output_cols, function(col) {
  data.frame(
    start        = jnt$start,
    end          = jnt$end,
    sample       = col,
    adj_sampen   = norm_det[[col]],
    norm_shannon = norm_det$shannon_norm,
    avg_meth     = norm_det$avg_meth,
    bc_vi        = jnt$bc_vi
  )
}))
## Attach per-cell methylation (same as unadjusted all.long)
all.long_adj <- cbind(jnt.adj.long, meth.long)

## Add adj summary columns to jnt, then save jnt_across_adj.rds
jnt$adj_sampen_avg <- rowMeans(norm_det[rownames(jnt), norm_output_cols])
jnt$shannon_norm   <- norm_det[rownames(jnt), "shannon_norm"]

## Adj grid performance plots
s_plots_adj <- make_grid_plots(S_GRID, "S", "bc_vi", data_dir, adj = TRUE)
n_plots_adj <- make_grid_plots(N_GRID, "N", "bc_vi", data_dir, adj = TRUE)
mi_fd_adj   <- mi_table_gen(jnt, "bc_vi", table = FALSE, adj = TRUE)

saveRDS(mi_fd_adj,    'simulations/rds/perf_across_adj.rds')
saveRDS(all.long_adj, 'simulations/rds/all.long_across_adj.rds')
saveRDS(jnt,          'simulations/rds/jnt_across_adj.rds')
saveRDS(s_plots_adj,  'simulations/rds/s_plots_across_adj.rds')
saveRDS(n_plots_adj,  'simulations/rds/n_plots_across_adj.rds')
```

## Plots

```{r samp_meth}
print(samp_meth)
```

```{r samp_vi}
print(samp_vi)
```

```{r samp_shan}
print(samp_shan)
```

```{r shannon_meth}
print(shannon_meth)
```

```{r shannon_vi}
print(shannon_vi)
```

```{r scmet_mu_vi}
print(scmet_mu_vi)
```

```{r scmet_mu_meth}
print(scmet_mu_meth)
```

```{r scmet_gamma_meth}
print(scmet_gamma_meth)
```

```{r scmet_epsilon_meth}
print(scmet_epsilon_meth)
```

```{r sampen_avg_vi}
print(sampen_avg_vi)
```

```{r sampen_avg_meth}
print(sampen_avg_meth)
```

## Performance

```{r nmi}
metric_labels <- c(
  "Sample Entropy" = "sample entropy",
  "Shannon Entropy" = "Shannon entropy",
  "Average Methylation" = "avg. methylation",
  "scMET (mu)" = expression("scMET" ~ mu),
  "scMET (gamma)" = expression("scMET" ~ gamma),
  "scMET (epsilon)" = expression("scMET" ~ epsilon)
)

nmi <- ggplot(mi_fd, aes(x = reorder(Metric, -NMI), y = NMI)) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(NMI, 3)), vjust = -0.5, size = 5) +
    theme_ng() +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = metric_labels) +
    labs(
        x = NULL,
        y = "Normalized Mutual Information (to acVI)"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 16))

print(nmi)
```

```{r s_nmi}
print(s_plots$nmi + xlab("# cells") + ylab("performance (metric NMI)"))
```

```{r s_seconds}
print(s_plots$seconds + xlab("# cells") + ylab("CPU time (s)"))
```

```{r s_mem}
print(s_plots$mem + xlab("# cells") + ylab("memory usage (MB)"))
```

```{r n_nmi}
print(n_plots$nmi + xlab("# features") + ylab("performance (metric NMI)"))
```

```{r n_seconds}
print(n_plots$seconds + xlab("# features") + ylab("CPU time (s)"))
```

```{r n_mem}
print(n_plots$mem + xlab("# features") + ylab("memory usage (MB)"))
```
